qoq_changes: Net sales increased 8.2% YoY to $4.63 billion.
Operating profit decreased 6.8% YoY to $784.2 million.
Operating, selling, general and administrative expenses increased 10.4% YoY to $1.58 billion.
new_risks: SG&A expense growth (10.4%) outpaced revenue growth (8.2%), leading to operating margin compression.
A newly recorded $98 million 'Other non-cash charge' weighed heavily on profitability metrics.
management_tone: Cautious, as solid top-line revenue growth was overshadowed by rising SG&A expenses and significant non-cash charges that drove a year-over-year decline in net income.
analyst_note: AutoZone demonstrated resilient demand with an 8.2% increase in net sales, but profitability was pressured by a 10.4% rise in SG&A and an unexplained $98 million non-cash charge. Investors should seek clarity on the nature of this non-cash charge and management's strategy to rein in operating expenses to protect margins.
